{"query":"Francis Ford Coppola Variety lawsuit $15 million libel September 2024 status","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1265,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/variety-asks-appeals-court-to-can-coppolas-defamation-suit-over-megalopolis-story/","title":"Variety asks appeals court to can Coppola's defamation suit over 'Megalopolis' story | Courthouse News Service","content":"Francis Ford Coppola has denied kissing or hugging topless extras.
LOS ANGELES (CN) — Hollywood magazine Variety asked the California Courts of Appeal on Wednesday to overrule the trial court and dismiss a defamation lawsuit brought by acclaimed film director Francis Ford Coppola over a news story about his 2024 movie, “Megalopolis.”
Months before its premier, the Guardian ran a lengthy story about the movie’s long and troubled production.
Two months later, Variety published an article headlined: “Video of Francis Ford Coppola Kissing ‘Megalopolis’ Extras Surfaces as Crew Members Detail Unprofessional Behavior on Set (EXCLUSIVE).” In addition to kissing extras, Variety reported that the octogenarian director was “often inadvertently inserting himself into the shot and ruining it."
Coppola sued Variety — but not the Guardian — over libel, denying he ever hugged or kissed an actress who was topless."},{"url":"https://people.com/francis-ford-coppola-sues-variety-libel-15-million-dollars-over-megalopolis-story-8710787","title":"Francis Ford Coppola Sues 'Variety' for $15 Million Over 'Megalopolis' Story","content":"A July 26 article published by 'Variety' alleged inappropriate behavior by the director towards female extras
11 and reviewed by PEOPLE, names Variety and executive editors Tatiana Siegel and Brent Lang.
Siegel and Lang appear on the byline for the article, which was initially published on July 26, 2024 and remains live on the Variety website.
Reps for Variety and its parent company PMC did not immediately respond to PEOPLE's request for comment.
Megalopolis is a sci-fi epic that stars Adam Driver, Nathalie Emmanuel, Giancarlo Esposito and DB Sweeney."},{"url":"https://www.rollingstone.com/tv-movies/tv-movie-news/francis-ford-coppola-sues-variety-megalopolis-1235099849/","title":"Francis Ford Coppola Sues 'Variety' for 'Megalopolis' Article","content":"Famed director Francis Ford Coppola has launched a $15 million libel lawsuit against Variety over the publication calling his behavior during the production of Megalopolis “unprofessional.”
In the lawsuit, filed in Los Angeles Superior Court on Wednesday against Variety Media LLC and other defendants and obtained by Rolling Stone, Coppola’s lawyers hail the 85-year-old as a “creative genius.” “Some people are jealous and resentful of genius,” the lawsuit says.
Coppola’s epic passion project Megalopolis is set for theatrical release on September 27."},{"url":"https://www.vulture.com/article/francis-ford-coppola-variety-megalopolis-libel.html","title":"Francis Ford Coppola Sues Variety for ‘Megalopolis’ ‘Libel’","content":"The director is now suing Variety magazine for libel over claims made in a July 26 article that Coppola had acted with “impunity” on the film’s set.
The director is seeking $15 million in damages from Variety Media LLC and the journalists who wrote the piece, Brent Lang and Tatiana Siegel, his lawyers said in a September 11 filing.
It was a collaboration of hundreds of artists, from extras to box office stars, to whom I consistently displayed the utmost respect and my deepest gratitude,” Coppola said in a statement shared with People on September 12.
Just two days prior, on September 9, Megalopolis extra Lauren Pagone, who was quoted by Variety, sued Coppola and others in Georgia for civil battery, civil assault, and negligent failure to prevent sexual harassment, per Deadline."}]}
